title: Prove scope commitment chain is unforgeable across delegation hops

artifacts.description: Augmented Delegation circuit that exposes previousScopeCommitment and currentScopeCommitment as public outputs and enforces: (1) scopeCommitment = Poseidon(scope, credCommitment), (2) delegatee permissions bits are a strict subset of delegator permission bits via cumulative AND-mask check.

Circom witness-gen adversarial harness (Mocha + snarkjs) that attempts to construct invalid witnesses: scope-expansion attack (delegatee bits exceed delegator bits), commitment-mismatch attack (previousScopeCommitment does not match prior hop output), and Poseidon pre-image substitution attack. Each case must fail constraint satisfaction.

Soundness property test: for all 2^8 pairs of (delegatorScope, delegateeScope), assert that witness generation succeeds iff delegateeScope & delegatorScope === delegateeScope (subset predicate). Uses exhaustive enumeration over the 8-bit permission space.

Append formal binding property statement: Binding(D): forall valid proof pi for hop i, Extract(pi).delegateeScope ⊆ Extract(pi).delegatorScope, and Poseidon(delegateeScope, credCommitment) = scopeCommitment_i. Reference collision-resistance assumption on Poseidon-128 and cite Grassi et al. 2021.

Security argument document: defines binding game, reduction to Poseidon collision resistance, proof that subset-check signal aliasing is impossible given circuit wiring, and documents adversarial test results as empirical evidence.

steps: Read circuits/src/Delegation.circom in full to map existing signal names: delegatorScope, delegateeScope, previousScopeCommitment, credCommitment, and all Poseidon component instantiations.

Identify the exact constraint that computes scopeCommitment = Poseidon(scope, credCommitment) and verify it feeds the same scope signal used in the AND-mask subset check — document any signal aliasing risk.

Add DelegationChainBinding.circom as a thin wrapper (or augmented copy) that promotes previousScopeCommitment and currentScopeCommitment to public outputs so the adversarial harness can inspect them without modifying the production circuit.

Write the adversarial harness: case 1 — set delegateeScope to delegatorScope | 0x01 (one extra bit) and assert calculateWitness throws ConstraintError. Case 2 — tamper previousScopeCommitment to a random field element and assert failure. Case 3 — swap the scope signal fed to Poseidon with a different value that still satisfies the AND-mask and assert commitment mismatch.

Write the exhaustive soundness test: iterate all 256×256 = 65536 (delegatorScope, delegateeScope) pairs, call calculateWitness, and assert success iff (delegateeScope & delegatorScope) === delegateeScope.
Run npm run test:circuits:fast to confirm all adversarial cases fail witness generation and all valid subset pairs pass.
Run npm run test:circuits:slow (FULL_PROOF=1) on a representative sample (16 valid pairs + 3 adversarial cases) to confirm the full Groth16 proof path rejects invalid witnesses.
Update circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md with the Binding(D) property statement, the Poseidon collision-resistance assumption, and cross-reference to adversarial test filenames.
Write docs/delegation-chain-security-proof.md covering: binding game definition, reduction sketch, wiring analysis, and test results table.
Commit all artifacts with DCO sign-off: git commit -s -m 'feat(circuits): formal binding harness for delegation scope commitment chain'
dependencies: circomlibjs (Poseidon JS implementation for off-circuit reference hashing in tests)
